strengths: Current hands-on agentic-AI infrastructure work at re:cinq — building MCP (Model Context Protocol) code generators and frameworks supporting agentic workflows, directly relevant to the role's multi-agent architecture mandate

Proven high-scale distributed-systems depth: low-latency microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second at dunnhumby/Sociomantic (async I/O, caching, real-time metadata), plus an event-driven, Kubernetes-deployable platform she founded and still operates

Regulated-fintech exposure in the German market — reshaping investment practices at RIDE Capital with disciplined engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and multi-country pricing-strategy optimization at Optilyz
concerns: No formal executive or people-leadership title on record — career history is senior/staff IC roles, with no documented track record of scaling an engineering org from 10 to 30+ or building a technical-leadership layer
Agentic-AI experience is recent (since April 2025) and centered on developer tooling and infrastructure rather than operating production multi-agent systems with direct financial consequences
No evidence of LLM selection, fine-tuning, or evaluation-methodology depth, nor of credit-systems or payment-flow infrastructure specifically

key_factors.factor: Deep distributed-systems and high-scale backend expertise
Fintech domain exposure in regulated environment

response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo brings nearly two decades of engineering experience that maps remarkably well onto this role's technical core: she is currently building the exact category of agentic-AI infrastructure (MCP generators, agentic-workflow frameworks) the company needs, and she has rare, battle-tested distributed-systems credentials — millions of requests per second, low-latency async architectures, event-driven platforms — that underpin the streaming and real-time-inference requirements. Her fintech chapters at RIDE Capital and Optilyz demonstrate she can apply craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, testability) in regulated, financially consequential domains, and her founder experience at GISCollective plus a sustained record of teaching, workshops, and community leadership evidence the ownership mindset, communication skill, and mentoring instinct a CTO needs. While her formal management scope is unproven, her breadth, current agentic focus, and production-hardened backend depth make her a technically credible, high-upside candidate for a hands-on executive role at a scale-up.
